participants.extracted: Adults aged 18 to 60 years presenting to the emergency department with a clinical suspicion of uncomplicated acute appendicitis at six Finnish hospitals (Turku, Tampere, Oulu University Hospitals, and Mikkeli, Jyväskylä, and Seinäjoki Central Hospitals). Patients were evaluated with history, examination, laboratory tests, urine analysis, and CT imaging; only those with CT-confirmed uncomplicated appendicitis were eligible. Key exclusions included pregnancy or lactation, contrast or iodine allergy, renal insufficiency, metformin use, peritonitis, inability to give informed consent or cooperate, serious systemic illness, and CT evidence of complicated appendicitis such as appendicolith, perforation, periappendicular abscess, or suspected tumor.

interventions.extracted: Participants randomized to the operative arm underwent standard open appendectomy through a McBurney right lower quadrant muscle-splitting incision, with preoperative prophylaxis of 1.5 g cefuroxime plus 500 mg metronidazole administered about 30 minutes before surgery. The appendix was removed and sent for histopathological examination. Participants randomized to the antibiotic arm received intravenous ertapenem 1 g daily for 3 days, starting in the emergency department, with clinical reassessment within 12 to 24 hours and monitoring during hospitalization. If appendicitis worsened or perforation/peritonitis was suspected, emergency appendectomy was performed. After the initial intravenous course, antibiotic therapy continued for 7 additional days with oral levofloxacin 500 mg once daily plus metronidazole 500 mg three times daily, for a total of 10 days; if ertapenem allergy was present, tazobactam 4 g three times daily plus metronidazole 500 mg three times daily was used intravenously, and if fluoroquinolone allergy was present, levofloxacin was replaced with cefalexin 500 mg three times daily or clindamycin 400 mg three times daily.

outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure the primary outcome of treatment success (treatment efficacy) at a minimum of 1 year: in the antibiotic arm, successful discharge without surgical intervention and without recurrent appendicitis during follow-up; in the appendectomy arm, successful appendectomy. Secondary outcomes included post-intervention complications (including surgical site infection/wound infection within 30 days after surgery, classified as superficial incisional, deep incisional, or organ/space SSI), late recurrence of acute appendicitis after conservative treatment (clinical diagnosis verified by appendectomy and histopathology), duration of hospital stay (days 0–2 during admission and overall length of stay), treatment costs over 12 months after randomization (direct medical and indirect productivity costs), post-intervention pain scores using a visual analog scale (VAS 0–10) and pain medication use at discharge, 1 week, and 2 months, sick leave during recovery and follow-up, mortality, and adverse reactions to antibiotics during the hospitalization period.
